text: The surgeon's left hand grasps the tissue at the area of demarcation, highlighting the boundary between different tissue regions.
The surgeon places the anvil through the fan and steel incision, which will later be used for specimen extraction.
The surgeon proceeds to divide the tissue, likely using surgical instruments to separate anatomical structures as part of the colectomy procedure.
The surgeon places a clip to the left of the stapler, observing pulsatile blood flow at the site, indicative of a successful cold knife cut test.
The surgeon proceeds to divide the specimen using surgical instruments, preparing it for extraction through the fan and steel incision.
The surgeon identifies the proximal margin of the specimen, preparing for division.
The surgeon closes the colotomy to prevent spillage of fecal matter or tumor cells while pulling the specimen through.
The surgeon uses a surgical stapler to staple the distal end of the bowel, securing the tissue in preparation for anastomosis.
The surgeon prepares to place the EEA stapler trans-anally, positioning it for the anastomosis procedure.
The surgeon demonstrates pulling the anterior staple line posteriorly, avoiding crossing staple lines during the procedure.
The surgeon brings the spike out from the stapler, preparing for the next step in the anastomosis process.
